meta.name: Subdivisions
meta.department: Public Works — Development Services Administration (DSA), Subdivision Section
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 18 (Subdivisions) — preliminary & final plat process, fees and who pays, how it ties to zoning, enforcement. Sourced to Municode + County Subdivision Processing Guidelines; hands off to MAPPS. First version, expanding.
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 18 (Subdivisions): a live parcel lookup, the preliminary & final plat process, the fees and who pays, how subdivision ties to your zoning district, and enforcement. Administered by the Subdivision Section, Public Works (DSA); the official application and review happen in MAPPS — this service explains the law and hands you off to MAPPS, it does not replace the County's record. Sections marked expanding are being filled in from the ingestion corpus; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: ★ Parcel lookup
sections.note: Before you subdivide, check the parcel. Your zoning district sets the minimum lot size and standards a subdivision must meet (e.g. the Agricultural district's 2-acre minimum, Ch. 19.30A), and a Special Management Area (SMA) or Important Ag Land designation adds review. Enter a Maui County TMK to see all of these, pulled live from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. For this parcel's actual subdivision/permit history (not in GIS), the result links you to MAPPS.

source.html: Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS — Parcels & Zoning. Subdivision filings via Maui County MAPPS (DSA Subdivision). No parcel data is stored or fabricated — every field is fetched live.

sections.title: 1 · How a subdivision works
sections.note: The path a subdivision takes under Title 18, administered by the Subdivision Section, Public Works (DSA). Applications are online through MAPPS.

steps.name: Create a MAPPS account & apply
steps.what: You must have a MAPPS Customer Self-Service (CSS) account to apply online; complete every step and upload the required documents and plans.
steps.pay: Expense: free to register · paid by: you (time)

steps.name: Submit the preliminary plat
steps.what: Required: a preliminary plat stamped by a Hawaiʻi-licensed land surveyor, a current title report, and an Approved Land Use Designation Form (applied through MAPPS).
steps.pay: Expense: surveyor + title report + filing fee · paid by: subdivider

steps.name: County review — 45 calendar days
steps.what: From the date the preliminary plat is deemed complete, the County has 45 calendar days to review and issue preliminary approval; the application is routed to all reviewing agencies.
steps.pay: Expense: County staff review · paid by: County (+ applicant fees)

steps.name: Preliminary approval
steps.what: Issued as a stamped plat (with any revisions noted) plus a letter listing the conditions to be met for final approval.
steps.pay: Expense: meeting conditions · paid by: subdivider

steps.name: Prepare & submit the final plat
steps.what: The subdivider shows all information required by Title 18 and addresses every comment on the approved preliminary letter and plat; the final plat is distributed to applicable agencies for their final recommendation.
steps.pay: Expense: revisions + any required improvements · paid by: subdivider

steps.name: Final subdivision approval
steps.what: Granted upon completion of all requirements — the subdivision may then be recorded.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: Where preliminary approval imposes a required improvement (an off-site road, drainage, or utility upgrade as a condition of subdividing), the County should bear the cost of the requirement it imposes — not download public-infrastructure cost onto the subdivider, who then prices it into housing.

source.html: County of Maui Subdivision Processing Guidelines (2022) + Subdivision Section; apply via MAPPS — DSA Subdivision ↗. Process summarized in plain language; exact requirements are in MCC Title 18 + the Guidelines.

sections.title: 2 · Subdivision fees — who pays
sections.note: Filing fees from the County's Subdivision Processing Guidelines. The subdivider pays; confirm current amounts with the Subdivision Section before relying on them.

source.html: Maui County Subdivision Processing Guidelines (DSA). Amounts are as published; verify the current schedule with the Subdivision Section, (808) 270-7253.
source.expanding: full fee schedule incl. time-extension & amendment fees

sections.title: 3 · What the Subdivision Section handles
sections.note: Beyond subdivisions, the same section administers related land matters.

rows.v: Dedicating a roadway to the County
Confirming legally separate lots of record

source.html: County of Maui Subdivision Section — administers MCC Title 18 + private easements, roadway dedication, and separate-lot determinations.

sections.title: 4 · How subdivision ties to zoning (Title 19)
sections.note: Subdivision and zoning are twin layers — Title 18 is the process, Title 19 is the standard it must meet.
sections.html: A subdivision must conform to the lot standards of the parcel's zoning district under Title 19. For example, the Agricultural district (Ch. 19.30A) sets a 2-acre minimum lot area, a 200-ft minimum lot width, and caps the number of lots per parcel by acreage (§19.30A.030(G)), with resubdivision limits at §19.30A.040 — so a Title 18 subdivision of ag land is bounded by those Title 19 numbers. Check your district's standards in the Title 19 service before filing.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 19.30A (ag lot standards, sourced in the Title 19 service) + MCC Title 18.

sections.title: 5 · Enforcement
sections.note: Subdivision violations are reachable through the same County enforcement engine as zoning and building. Not legal advice.
sections.html:
Title 19's enforcement chapter, §19.530.030, expressly reaches violations of Maui County Code titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 plus related rules and permits — so a Title 18 subdivision violation (e.g. selling lots from an unapproved subdivision) can be pursued by a director through a notice of violation and order (cease-and-desist, correction by a date, and/or civil fines), final after 30 days unless appealed to the Board of Variances and Appeals.

The assessment schedule is Rule §15-2 (Title MC-15, eff. 9/3/1993) — the one cross-title rule for Buildings (16), Subdivisions (18) & Zoning (19). Subdivision-relevant civil fines (initial / per-day): a development standard (lot size/width, frontage, etc.) $1,000 / $100; permit conditions $1,000 / $100; misrepresentation on an application/plan $1,000 / $100. Continuing violation: the daily fine doubles each 30-day period (max $1,000/day); appeals to the Board of Variances and Appeals or Board of Code Appeals. Full cross-title schedule in the Title 19 service §4B ↗.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 19.530 (Enforcement) — §19.530.030 reach + the implementing Rule §15-2 (MC-15, mauicounty.gov ↗) covering Titles 16/18/19; full table in the Title 19 service §4B.

sections.title: 6 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — the same process read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, Public Works (DSA) administers subdivision approval and may condition approval on required improvements (roads, drainage, utilities) to serve the new lots; filing and review fees are set by ordinance / the Guidelines. The Charter frames subdivision as a delegated administrative approval funded by applicant fees plus subdivider-built infrastructure.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, subdividing is the owner exercising a right in their own land. Where the County conditions approval on public infrastructure it would otherwise build, that cost should be County-borne or shared — exacting it from the subdivider quietly raises the price of every resulting home and works against local housing.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Fees that recover the cost of review are fair. Required off-site improvements — public infrastructure the County would otherwise fund — are where cost should shift back to the County, so subdivision doesn't become a hidden housing tax. Same process, read two ways.

sections.title: 7 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification
sections.intro: Provisions that live in the subdivision guidelines / practice that, in our assessment, should be codified into Title 18 — so the binding standard is council-adopted and publicly noticed. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
items: The 45-day review clock. The preliminary-review timeline lives in the Processing Guidelines. → Codify a review SLA in Title 18 so applicants have an enforceable expectation, not a practice that can drift.

Required-improvement cost allocation. Whether a subdivider must build (and pay for) off-site public infrastructure is decided case-by-case as a condition. → Codify a cost-allocation rule (what the County funds vs. the subdivider) so the housing-cost impact is transparent. expanding
Separate-lot determination criteria. The standard for confirming a legally separate lot of record is administrative. → Codify the criteria so determinations are consistent and appealable.

source.html: Analysis grounded in MCC Title 18 + the Subdivision Processing Guidelines; guideline contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the process navigator, and the fee table are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a saved subdivision-readiness checklist, a document-package helper, and an AI subdivision assistant for Title 18 will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only).
